purpose: Pin the contract for the narrow asupersync Lab-backed replay prototype produced by `frankenlibc_harness::asupersync_lab_replay::validate_replay` + `classify_outcome`. The prototype targets ONE workload-trace class (`stdio.fread_small`) and reports tool failures separately from code failures so a missing or broken `/dp/asupersync` install never masquerades as a regression in frankenlibc itself.

narrow_scope.rationale: Smallest deterministic workload trace with a real FILE-membrane lock geometry — sufficient to prove the schema + tool-failure routing without requiring a broad workload suite.

outcome_classification.rationale: Tool failures and code failures are reported with mutually exclusive variants so a downstream dashboard can weight them differently. A missing asupersync install must NEVER produce CodeFailure.

fallback_path.rationale: When asupersync is unavailable the replay record is still validated; the gate emits ToolFailure with the trace_class and a structured reason so a CI or dashboard can surface a tool-install issue separately from a frankenlibc code regression.
fallback_path.tool_failure_reason_format: asupersync tool unavailable (trace_class=<id>); cannot validate virtual-time replay

detection_contract.rationale: Every caller MUST resolve `asupersync_available` through `detect_asupersync_available(&DetectionEnv)` so the probe order is deterministic and inspectable. The `detection_reason` enum-string is the only legitimate way to attribute a Pass / CodeFailure / ToolFailure outcome back to an environment state.

downstream_consumers.consumes: When ToolFailure fires, the trace minimizer treats the run as inconclusive — not as a strict/hardened divergence.
The explain dossier surfaces ToolFailure under the `support_taxonomy_claim` section, NOT as a `first_failing_blocker`.
